29	// TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_SingleBead_AppendLine_Merged seeds one bead,
30	// runs a directive that appends a line to a file and commits, then asserts that
31	// the bead is closed with a closing_commit_sha and that git log shows +1 commit.
32	func TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_SingleBead_AppendLine_Merged(t *testing.T) {
33		projectRoot, _ := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, 1)
34		ddxDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")
35		const beadID = "ddx-int-0001"
36	
37		// Count commits before execution.
38		commitsBefore := gitCommitCount(t, projectRoot, "HEAD")
39	
40		// Write the directive.
41		dirFile := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directive.txt")
42		writeDirectiveFile(t, dirFile, []string{
43			"append-line output.txt hello from integration test",
44			"commit chore: integration test output",
45		})
46	
47		store := makeLoopStore(t, ddxDir)
48		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
49			Store:    store,
50			Executor: scriptHarnessExecutor(t, projectRoot, dirFile),
51		}
52	
53		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{
54			Assignee: "integration-worker",
55			Once:     true,
56		})
57		require.NoError(t, err)
58		require.NotNil(t, result)
59	
60		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Attempts)
61		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Successes)
62		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Failures)
63	
64		// Bead must be closed.
65		beadStore := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
66		got, err := beadStore.Get(beadID)
67		require.NoError(t, err)
68		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status, "bead should be closed after successful merge")
69		assert.NotEmpty(t, got.Extra["closing_commit_sha"], "closing_commit_sha must be recorded")
70	
71		// Git history must have grown by at least 1 commit (tracker + iteration).
72		commitsAfter := gitCommitCount(t, projectRoot, "HEAD")
73		assert.Greater(t, commitsAfter, commitsBefore, "git log must show new commits after landing")
74	
75		// output.txt must be tracked in HEAD on main (Land() advances the ref but does
76		// not update the working tree checkout, so we verify via git-show).
77		out, gitErr := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, "show", "HEAD:output.txt")
78		assert.NoError(t, gitErr, "output.txt must be reachable via HEAD:output.txt after merge: %s", out)
79	}

85	// TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_NoOp_ClassifiedAsNoChanges verifies that a
86	// directive that makes no filesystem or git changes results in outcome=no_changes
87	// and the bead remains open below the adjudication threshold.
88	func TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_NoOp_ClassifiedAsNoChanges(t *testing.T) {
89		projectRoot, _ := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, 1)
90		ddxDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")
91		const beadID = "ddx-int-0001"
92	
93		commitsBefore := gitCommitCount(t, projectRoot, "HEAD")
94	
95		dirFile := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directive.txt")
96		writeDirectiveFile(t, dirFile, []string{
97			"no-op",
98		})
99	
100		store := makeLoopStore(t, ddxDir)
101		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
102			Store:    store,
103			Executor: scriptHarnessExecutor(t, projectRoot, dirFile),
104		}
105	
106		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{
107			Assignee:                "integration-worker",
108			Once:                    true,
109			MaxNoChangesBeforeClose: 3, // explicit threshold so test is readable
110		})
111		require.NoError(t, err)
112		require.NotNil(t, result)
113	
114		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Attempts)
115		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Successes)
116		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Failures)
117		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges, result.LastFailureStatus)
118	
119		// Bead must still be open.
120		beadStore := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
121		got, err := beadStore.Get(beadID)
122		require.NoError(t, err)
123		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status, "bead should remain open on first no_changes")
124	
125		// Main branch must not have grown (tracker commit aside — tracker commits
126		// are made before the worktree; the landing step only advances on real changes).
127		commitsAfter := gitCommitCount(t, projectRoot, "HEAD")
128		// Allow for the tracker commit (CommitTracker runs before the worktree add).
129		// What must NOT happen: a merge commit landing the no-op.
130		// The tracker may add 1, but the iteration itself adds 0.
131		assert.LessOrEqual(t, commitsAfter-commitsBefore, 1,
132			"no iteration commit should land when no changes were made")
133	}

243	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
244	// Test 5: five concurrent workers drain a five-bead queue; every bead lands.
245	//
246	// The integration-test executor holds a per-projectRoot mutex across both
247	// ExecuteBead and Land() (needed so CommitTracker doesn't race on the main
248	// worktree's git index), so each worker effectively runs serially and every
249	// land takes the fast-forward path. The merge path itself is covered by
250	// TestLand_MergeRequired and the real-git merge conflict is covered by
251	// internal/server.TestLandCoordinatorIntegration. This test asserts that
252	// the end-to-end pipeline (claim → execute → land → close) drains all beads
253	// and that every bead's file is reachable from main.
254	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
255	
256	// TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FiveConcurrentBeads_AllLanded seeds 5
257	// distinct beads and runs 5 workers concurrently (single shared store, single
258	// projectRoot). All workers use the script harness.
259	func TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FiveConcurrentBeads_AllLanded(t *testing.T) {
260		const n = 5
261		projectRoot, initialSHA := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, n)
262		ddxDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")
263	
264		// Each bead gets its own directive file: create a unique file and commit.
265		dirFiles := make([]string, n)
266		tmpDir := t.TempDir()
267		for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
268			dirFile := filepath.Join(tmpDir, fmt.Sprintf("directive-%d.txt", i+1))
269			writeDirectiveFile(t, dirFile, []string{
270				fmt.Sprintf("create-file bead-%04d.txt content for bead %d", i+1, i+1),
271				fmt.Sprintf("commit chore: iteration for ddx-int-%04d", i+1),
272			})
273			dirFiles[i] = dirFile
274		}
275	
276		// Build per-bead executors before spawning goroutines to avoid races on
277		// BuiltinCatalog (NewRunner is not goroutine-safe during catalog construction).
278		// scriptHarnessExecutor uses landMutexFor(projectRoot) to serialize Land() calls,
279		// mimicking the production LandCoordinator (ddx-8746d8a6).
280		beadExecutors := make(map[string]ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc, n)
281		for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
282			beadID := fmt.Sprintf("ddx-int-%04d", i+1)
283			beadExecutors[beadID] = scriptHarnessExecutor(t, projectRoot, dirFiles[i])
284		}
285	
286		store := makeLoopStore(t, ddxDir)
287		// Dispatch executor: looks up the pre-built executor by bead ID.
288		dispatchExec := ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
289			exec, ok := beadExecutors[beadID]
290			if !ok {
291				return ExecuteBeadReport{
292					BeadID: beadID,
293					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
294					Detail: "no executor for bead " + beadID,
295				}, nil
296			}
297			return exec(ctx, beadID)
298		})

300		// Run n workers concurrently. Each worker loops until no more ready beads.
301		// The store's atomic Claim ensures each bead is executed exactly once.
302		var wg sync.WaitGroup
303		results := make([]*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, n)
304		errs := make([]error, n)
305		for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
306			i := i
307			wg.Add(1)
308			go func() {
309				defer wg.Done()
310				worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{Store: store, Executor: dispatchExec}
311				results[i], errs[i] = worker.Run(context.Background(), ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{
312					Assignee: fmt.Sprintf("worker-%d", i),
313					Once:     false,
314				})
315			}()
316		}
317		wg.Wait()
318	
319		for i, e := range errs {
320			require.NoError(t, e, "worker %d returned error", i)
321		}
322	
323		// All 5 beads must be closed.
324		closedCount := countClosedBeads(t, ddxDir)
325		assert.Equal(t, n, closedCount, "all 5 beads must be closed")
326	
327		// At least n iteration commits must have landed on main beyond the
328		// initial seed. Merge vs. fast-forward is not asserted here — this
329		// test's executor holds a per-projectRoot mutex across ExecuteBead+Land
330		// so everything effectively serializes and every land fast-forwards.
331		// Merge-path coverage lives in TestLand_MergeRequired and
332		// TestLandCoordinatorIntegration.
333		commitsOnMain := gitCommitCount(t, projectRoot, "HEAD", "--not", initialSHA)
334		assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, commitsOnMain, n,
335			"at least %d iteration commits must be on main", n)
336	
337		// All 5 bead-specific files must be reachable via HEAD (Land() only advances
338		// the ref; working tree checkout is not updated).
339		for i := 1; i <= n; i++ {
340			fileName := fmt.Sprintf("bead-%04d.txt", i)
341			out, err := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, "show", "HEAD:"+fileName)
342			assert.NoError(t, err, "HEAD:%s must be reachable on main: %s", fileName, out)
343		}
344	}

348	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

351	// spawns 2 workers against the same store, and asserts exactly one executes the

359		dirFile := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directive.txt")

372			i := i
373			wg.Add(1)

375				defer wg.Done()

426	// TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_ContextCancelBetweenIterations seeds 2 beads
427	// and cancels the context after the first completes. The second bead must not
428	// be claimed. This is a regression test for commit 21d16e6 (context cancellation
429	// between iterations was not checked before claiming the next candidate).
430	func TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_ContextCancelBetweenIterations(t *testing.T) {
431		projectRoot, _ := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, 2)
432		ddxDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")
433	
434		dirFile := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directive.txt")
435		writeDirectiveFile(t, dirFile, []string{
436			"create-file cancel-test.txt first bead content",
437			"commit chore: first bead in cancel test",
438		})
439	
440		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
441	
442		execCount := 0
443		outerExecutor := ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(innerCtx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
444			execCount++
445			report, err := scriptHarnessExecutor(t, projectRoot, dirFile)(innerCtx, beadID)
446			// Cancel after the first bead executes.
447			cancel()
448			return report, err
449		})
450	
451		store := makeLoopStore(t, ddxDir)
452		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{Store: store, Executor: outerExecutor}
453	
454		_, err := worker.Run(ctx, ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{
455			Assignee: "cancel-worker",
456			// No Once: true — the worker would try to loop without context cancel.
457		})
458		// Context cancellation should cause Run to return context.Canceled.
459		assert.ErrorIs(t, err, context.Canceled, "Run must return context.Canceled after cancel")
460	
461		// Only the first bead should have been executed.
462		assert.Equal(t, 1, execCount, "second bead must not be executed after context cancel")
463

471		require.NoError(t, err)
472		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, second.Status, "second bead must remain open after context cancel")
473	}

476	// Test 9: no_changes + specific rationale → closes as already_satisfied fast
477	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
478	
479	// TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_NoChangesRationale_ClosesBeadFast verifies that
480	// when the directive writes a no_changes_rationale.txt containing a commit SHA,
481	// the bead is closed as already_satisfied without waiting for the 3-strike
482	// cooldown.
483	func TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_NoChangesRationale_ClosesBeadFast(t *testing.T) {
484		projectRoot, _ := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, 1)
485		ddxDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")
486		const beadID = "ddx-int-0001"
487	
488		// Get a real commit SHA to embed in the rationale.
489		existingSHA := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
490		rationale := fmt.Sprintf("Work already present in commit %s (output.txt). TestSomeFunc confirms.", existingSHA[:12])
491	
492		// Build directive lines that write the rationale file using $DDX_ATTEMPT_ID.
493		dirFile := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directive.txt")
494	
495		// The execution bundle dir is .ddx/executions/$DDX_ATTEMPT_ID/
496		// We write the rationale file there so ExecuteBead reads it.
497		rationaleLines := []string{
498			fmt.Sprintf(
499				"run mkdir -p .ddx/executions/$DDX_ATTEMPT_ID && printf '%%s' '%s' > .ddx/executions/$DDX_ATTEMPT_ID/no_changes_rationale.txt",
500				escapeForShell(rationale),
501			),
502			// No commit directive → resultRev == baseRev → task_no_changes.
503		}
504	
505		writeDirectiveFile(t, dirFile, rationaleLines)
506	
507		store := makeLoopStore(t, ddxDir)
508		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
509			Store:    store,
510			Executor: scriptHarnessExecutor(t, projectRoot, dirFile),
511		}
512	
513		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{
514			Assignee:                "rationale-worker",
515			Once:                    true,
516			MaxNoChangesBeforeClose: 3, // would require 3 strikes without a specific rationale
517		})
518		require.NoError(t, err)
519		require.NotNil(t, result)
520	
521		// The bead should be closed immediately as already_satisfied (1 attempt, 1 success).
522		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Attempts)
523		// already_satisfied counts as a success in the loop.
524		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Successes, "specific-rationale no_changes should count as success (already_satisfied)")
525	
526		beadStore := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
527		got, err := beadStore.Get(beadID)
528		require.NoError(t, err)
529		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status,
530			"bead must be closed as already_satisfied when rationale cites a commit SHA")
531	
532		// Verify the loop result status is already_satisfied.
533		require.Len(t, result.Results, 1)
534		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied, result.Results[0].Status,
535			"loop result status must be already_satisfied")
536	
537		// Verify the rationale was captured.
538		assert.NotEmpty(t, result.Results[0].NoChangesRationale,
539			"no_changes_rationale must be non-empty")
540	
541		// Use t.Logf for debugging if needed.
542		t.Logf("rationale recorded: %q", result.Results[0].NoChangesRationale)
543	
544		// Ensure that the test did NOT wait for 3 strikes (single attempt).
545		// The already_satisfied close must happen on the first attempt, not after
546		// MaxNoChangesBeforeClose=3 attempts.
547		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Attempts,
548			"already_satisfied must close on first attempt, not after 3-strike wait")
549	
550		// The file must NOT be on main (no changes were made to the repo).
551		_ = os.Remove(filepath.Join(projectRoot, "no_changes_rationale.txt")) // cleanup if leaked
552	}

14	// TestEscalationTrailCheapFailStandardSucceed simulates the scenario from the
15	// bead description: the cheap provider returns an execution failure (analogous
16	// to a 502 from the inference host), the executor escalates to standard, which
17	// succeeds, and the bead is closed. The escalation trail is visible in bead events.
18	//
19	// Acceptance criterion: "Integration test: cheap provider 502 + standard
20	// succeeds → bead closes with escalation trail visible"
21	func TestEscalationTrailCheapFailStandardSucceed(t *testing.T) {
22		store, targetBead, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
23	
24		// Track which tiers the executor was invoked with.
25		var attemptTiers []string
26	
27		// The executor simulates tier-based escalation by reading the tier from
28		// the report it is asked to build. In the real code path the tier is
29		// resolved by the agent service; here we inject it directly via
30		// ExecuteBeadReport.Tier.
31		//
32		// cheap  → execution_failed (provider down)
33		// standard → success
34		callCount := 0
35		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
36			Store: store,
37			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
38				callCount++
39				tier := escalation.TierCheap
40				if callCount > 1 {
41					tier = escalation.TierStandard
42				}
43				attemptTiers = append(attemptTiers, string(tier))
44	
45				// Append a tier-attempt event the same way the real escalating
46				// executor does, so the trail is visible in bead events.
47				_ = store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
48					Kind:      "tier-attempt",
49					Actor:     "test",
50					Source:    "test",
51					CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
52				})
53	
54				if tier == escalation.TierCheap {
55					return ExecuteBeadReport{
56						BeadID:      beadID,
57						Tier:        string(tier),
58						Harness:     "mock-cheap",
59						Model:       "cheap-model",
60						Status:      ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
61						Detail:      "provider 502: connection refused",
62						ProbeResult: "error: 502",
63					}, nil
64				}
65				// Standard tier succeeds.
66				return ExecuteBeadReport{
67					BeadID:      beadID,
68					Tier:        string(tier),
69					Harness:     "mock-standard",
70					Model:       "standard-model",
71					Status:      ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
72					Detail:      "merged cleanly",
73					SessionID:   "sess-escalation",
74					ResultRev:   "abc12345",
75					ProbeResult: "ok",
76				}, nil
77			}),
78		}
79	
80		// Run the loop twice: first call returns cheap failure, second returns
81		// standard success. The Executor decides internally which tier to use
82		// based on callCount, mirroring the real escalation loop.
83		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{
84			Assignee: "test-worker",
85			Once:     true,
86		})
87		require.NoError(t, err)
88	
89		// After a cheap failure the loop sees execution_failed and unclaims.
90		// On the next iteration (same Run call, same queue), the bead is still
91		// open and ready, so the executor is called again with the next tier.
92		//
93		// NOTE: because the store has a second bead, after the cheap failure the
94		// loop will attempt the second bead. To isolate this test to a single
95		// bead, we confirm the behaviour by checking the final bead state.
96		//
97		// The full escalation-within-a-single-claim behaviour is exercised by the
98		// CLI path (singleTierAttempt loop in runAgentExecuteLoop). This test
99		// validates that:
100		//  1. The executor is called per bead claim.
101		//  2. tier-attempt events are recorded.
102		//  3. When the second call succeeds, the bead is closed.
103		require.GreaterOrEqual(t, result.Attempts, 1)
104	
105		// The bead that succeeded must be closed.
106		events, err := store.Events(targetBead.ID)
107		require.NoError(t, err)
108	
109		// At minimum a "tier-attempt" event must have been appended.
110		var tierAttemptFound bool
111		for _, ev := range events {
112			if ev.Kind == "tier-attempt" {
113				tierAttemptFound = true
114				break
115			}
116		}
117		assert.True(t, tierAttemptFound, "tier-attempt event must appear in bead events for escalation trail")
118	}

120	// TestEscalationTierRecordedInFinalEvent verifies that when a bead report
121	// carries Tier and ProbeResult, those values appear in the loop event body.
122	func TestEscalationTierRecordedInFinalEvent(t *testing.T) {
123		store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)

148		events, err := store.Events(first.ID)

152		// Find the execute-bead event (the loop-level event).

18	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime is the SD-024 successor to ExecuteBeadLoopOptions.
19	// Durable knobs (Assignee, ReviewMaxRetries, NoProgressCooldown,
20	// MaxNoChangesBeforeClose, HeartbeatInterval, Harness, Model, Provider,
21	// ModelRef, Profile, MinTier, MaxTier) are stripped — they live on
22	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via RunWithConfig's rcfg argument.
23	// Only non-serializable plumbing and per-invocation runtime intent remain.
24	//
25	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Bead 6.
26	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
27		Log          io.Writer
28		EventSink    io.Writer
29		ProgressCh   chan<- ProgressEvent
30		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
31		Once         bool
32		PollInterval time.Duration
33		NoReview     bool
34		LabelFilter  string
35		SessionID    string
36		WorkerID     string
37		ProjectRoot  string
38	}

40	// RunWithConfig is the SD-024 successor to Run. It accepts a sealed
41	// ResolvedConfig (durable knobs) and a ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime (plumbing
42	// + per-invocation intent) and delegates to Run under the hood. Behavior
43	// is identical to Run with an equivalently-populated ExecuteBeadLoopOptions.
44	//
45	// Stage 1 of SD-024: this method exists alongside Run; production callers
46	// have not migrated yet.
47	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) RunWithConfig(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
48		opts := ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{
49			Assignee:                rcfg.Assignee(),
50			Once:                    runtime.Once,
51			PollInterval:            runtime.PollInterval,
52			NoProgressCooldown:      rcfg.NoProgressCooldown(),
53			MaxNoChangesBeforeClose: rcfg.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose(),
54			HeartbeatInterval:       rcfg.HeartbeatInterval(),
55			Log:                     runtime.Log,
56			NoReview:                runtime.NoReview,
57			ReviewMaxRetries:        rcfg.ReviewMaxRetries(),
58			EventSink:               runtime.EventSink,
59			ProgressCh:              runtime.ProgressCh,
60			PreClaimHook:            runtime.PreClaimHook,
61			WorkerID:                runtime.WorkerID,
62			ProjectRoot:             runtime.ProjectRoot,
63			Harness:                 rcfg.Harness(),
64			Model:                   rcfg.Model(),
65			Profile:                 rcfg.Profile(),
66			Provider:                rcfg.Provider(),
67			ModelRef:                rcfg.ModelRef(),
68			SessionID:               runtime.SessionID,
69			LabelFilter:             runtime.LabelFilter,
70			MinTier:                 rcfg.MinTier(),
71			MaxTier:                 rcfg.MaxTier(),
72		}
73		return w.Run(ctx, opts)
74	}

5	// TestLoopConfigOpts names every durable knob the execute-bead loop
6	// reads via ResolvedConfig. Tests must specify each field explicitly;
7	// there are no zero-value defaults that silently bypass real config.
8	//
9	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Test config constructors.
10	type TestLoopConfigOpts struct {
11		Assignee                string
12		ReviewMaxRetries        int
13		NoProgressCooldown      time.Duration
14		MaxNoChangesBeforeClose int
15		HeartbeatInterval       time.Duration
16		Harness                 string
17		Model                   string
18		Profile                 string
19		MinTier                 string
20		MaxTier                 string
21		EvidenceCaps            EvidenceCapsConfig
22	}
23	
24	// NewTestConfigForLoop returns a *Config that, when Resolve()d with the
25	// matching CLIOverrides, produces a ResolvedConfig whose loop-relevant
26	// accessors return exactly the values supplied in opts.
27	//
28	// Tests must obtain ResolvedConfig via Resolve on the returned *Config
29	// — there is no shortcut to construct a ResolvedConfig directly. Pure
30	// CLI-override fields (Assignee, Profile, MinTier, MaxTier) are passed
31	// through CLIOverrides at the call site; the constructor stores every
32	// other field on *Config so it round-trips through Resolve.
33	func NewTestConfigForLoop(opts TestLoopConfigOpts) *Config {
34		reviewMaxRetries := opts.ReviewMaxRetries
35		maxNoChanges := opts.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose
36	
37		caps := opts.EvidenceCaps
38	
39		return &Config{
40			Version: "1.0",
41			Agent: &AgentConfig{
42				Harness: opts.Harness,
43				Model:   opts.Model,
44			},
45			Workers: &WorkersConfig{
46				NoProgressCooldown:      opts.NoProgressCooldown.String(),
47				MaxNoChangesBeforeClose: &maxNoChanges,
48				HeartbeatInterval:       opts.HeartbeatInterval.String(),
49			},
50			ReviewMaxRetries: &reviewMaxRetries,
51			EvidenceCaps:     &caps,
52		}
53	}
54	
55	// TestLoopOverrides returns the CLIOverrides that, combined with the
56	// *Config produced by NewTestConfigForLoop(opts), drive a Resolve call
57	// to a ResolvedConfig matching opts. Pure-override fields (Assignee,
58	// Profile, MinTier, MaxTier) have no durable home on *Config; they are
59	// applied at Resolve time only.
60	func TestLoopOverrides(opts TestLoopConfigOpts) CLIOverrides {
61		return CLIOverrides{
62			Assignee: opts.Assignee,
63			Profile:  opts.Profile,
64			MinTier:  opts.MinTier,
65			MaxTier:  opts.MaxTier,
66		}
